title: Connection refused on localhost for YouTube OAuth2
symptom: When attempting to access n8n on localhost:5678, the connection is refused. After changing the host port to 7889, the OAuth2 redirect URL still uses port 5678, leading to connection issues.
not_this_if: Using the default port 5678 without changing it
root_cause: The issue arises because n8n auto-generates the OAuth2 redirect URL using the default internal port (5678) instead of the user-defined host port. This leads to a mismatch when the user tries to authenticate, resulting in a connection refusal.
fix_steps: Set the environment variable N8N_PORT to your desired host port (e.g., 7889)
Update the OAuth2 credentials in Google to use the correct redirect URL with the new port
Ensure that the webhook URLs are configured correctly as per n8n documentation
agent_instruction: Change the N8N_PORT environment variable and update the OAuth2 redirect URL accordingly.
